docs.title: NopAccelerate AI Analytics
docs.text:
The NopAccelerate AI Analytics plugin brings AI-powered business intelligence directly into your nopCommerce admin panel. Ask questions about your store's performance in plain English and get instant, data-driven answers — no SQL, no exports, no dashboards to learn.

Once configured, the plugin provides:

  • A visual Analytics Dashboard with revenue trends, order trends, KPI cards, and top product rankings
  • An Ask AI chat interface where you can query your store data conversationally
  • Persistent chat history — your conversation is saved and restored across sessions
  • Excel export of dashboard data with multiple sheets
  • Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, and custom AI providers
Plugin Name NopAccelerate AI Analytics Version 1.00 Author XcellenceIt Compatible With nopCommerce 4.90 System Name Widgets.AIAnalytics

Next →

docs.title: Version 1.00
docs.text: Release Date 2026 Plug-In Version 1.00 Supported nopCommerce Version 4.90

docs.title: Features:
docs.text:
1. Analytics Dashboard with KPI cards — Total Revenue, Total Orders, Active Customers, Average Order Value.
  2. Revenue Trend (line chart) and Orders Trend (bar chart) across the full selected date range with zero-fill for days with no activity.
  3. Top 10 Products by Revenue ranked table with order count per product.
  4. Ask AI conversational chat interface — ask plain-English questions about store data and receive AI-generated answers.
  5. Persistent chat history stored in database — conversation survives page refresh and browser restart.
  6. Multi-provider AI support: OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter (Gemini, LLaMA, Mistral, and others), Azure OpenAI, Custom endpoint.
  7. Excel export with three sheets: Summary KPIs, Daily Revenue, Top Products.
  8. Date range and store filtering with quick-select Last 7 Days / Last 30 Days shortcuts.
  9. Test Connection button on Settings page to verify AI provider credentials.
  10. Privacy toggle to control external data transmission.
  11. Full localization support via nopCommerce locale resources.
  12. Persistent DB table for chat history with install/uninstall migration.

docs.title: Ask AI
docs.text:
The Ask AI page provides a conversational chat interface where you can ask plain-English questions about your store's data and receive instant AI-generated answers.

Access it from NopBot AI Analytics → Ask AI in the admin sidebar, or via the Ask AI button on the Dashboard.

docs.title: Filter Bar
docs.text:
The same date range and store filters from the Dashboard are available here. The AI uses these filters when querying your store data — for example, asking "What was my total revenue?" will return revenue for the selected period only.

Control Description Date From Start of the data period the AI will use to answer questions. Date To End of the data period. Store Filter to a specific store or use All Stores. Clear Chat Deletes the full conversation history from the database permanently. A confirmation prompt appears before deletion.

docs.title: Suggested Prompts
docs.text:
A row of prompt chips appears below the filter bar to help you get started:

  • What was my total revenue this month?
  • Which 5 products generated the most revenue?
  • How many new customers placed orders?
  • What is the average order value?
  • Show daily revenue as a table

Click any chip to populate the input field, then press Enter or the send button to submit.

docs.title: Chat Interface
docs.text:
Type your question in the input field at the bottom of the page. Press Enter to send, or Shift+Enter to add a new line within your question.

The AI receives your store's aggregated metrics as context (total revenue, order count, top products, daily revenue breakdown) and generates a relevant answer. If the answer contains tabular data, it is rendered as a formatted table within the chat bubble.

docs.title: Chat History Persistence
docs.text:
Your conversation is automatically saved to the database after each exchange. When you return to the Ask AI page — even after a page refresh or closing the browser — your previous messages are restored and displayed in the chat panel.

To permanently delete your conversation history, click Clear Chat. This removes all records from the database for your admin account.

docs.title: Example Questions
docs.text:
Question What the AI returns What was my total revenue this month? The total revenue figure for the selected date range. Which 5 products generated the most revenue? A table with product names and revenue values. How many new customers placed orders? The unique customer count for the period. What is the average order value? The AOV calculated from your filtered orders. Show daily revenue as a table A table with one row per day showing the revenue amount.

Note: The AI can only answer questions based on the store data provided in its context (orders, revenue, customers, products). It cannot access product descriptions, inventory levels, or customer personal details.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The Dashboard is the main analytics view, accessible from NopBot AI Analytics → Dashboard in the admin sidebar.

docs.title: Filter Bar
docs.text:
At the top of the dashboard you will find the filter controls:

Control Description Date From Start date for the analytics period. Defaults to 29 days ago. Date To End date for the analytics period. Defaults to today. Store Filter by a specific store. Defaults to "All Stores" in multi-store setups. Refresh Submit the filter form and reload all charts and KPI cards for the selected period. Last 7 days Quick-select shortcut — sets the date range to the last 7 days and refreshes automatically. Last 30 days Quick-select shortcut — sets the date range to the last 30 days and refreshes automatically. Export Excel Downloads a .xlsx file with three sheets: Summary KPIs, Daily Revenue, and Top Products.

docs.title: KPI Cards
docs.text:
Four summary cards appear at the top of the dashboard:

Card Description Total Revenue Sum of all non-cancelled order totals in the selected period. Total Orders Count of all non-cancelled orders in the selected period. Active Customers Count of unique customers who placed at least one order in the selected period. Avg. Order Value Total Revenue divided by Total Orders.

docs.title: Revenue Trend
docs.text: A line chart showing daily revenue across every day in the selected date range. Days with no orders show as zero — the full timeline is always rendered so you can spot gaps clearly.

docs.title: Orders Trend
docs.text: A bar chart showing the number of orders placed per day across the same date range.

docs.title: Top 10 Products by Revenue
docs.text:
A ranked table listing the top 10 products by total revenue in the selected period.

Column Description # Rank (1 = highest revenue). Product Product name. Orders Total quantity sold across all orders. Revenue Total revenue generated by this product.

docs.title: Excel Export
docs.text:
Clicking Export Excel downloads a file named AIAnalytics_Dashboard_YYYYMMDD.xlsx containing:

  • Sheet 1 — Summary: Period dates and the four KPI values.
  • Sheet 2 — Daily Revenue: One row per day with Date, Revenue, and Orders columns.
  • Sheet 3 — Top Products: Full product ranking with name, order count, and revenue.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Analytics Dashboard – Visual KPI cards showing Total Revenue, Total Orders, Active Customers, and Average Order Value for any selected date range.
  • Revenue & Orders Trend Charts – Interactive line and bar charts showing day-by-day performance across the full selected period.
  • Top 10 Products by Revenue – Ranked table showing your best-performing products with order count and revenue figures.
  • Ask AI Chat – Conversational AI interface that answers plain-English questions about your store data (e.g. "Which 5 products generated the most revenue?").
  • Persistent Chat History – Ask AI conversations are saved to the database and restored automatically when you return to the page.
  • Excel Export – Export dashboard data as a multi-sheet .xlsx file covering Summary KPIs, Daily Revenue, and Top Products.
  • Multi-Provider AI Support – Connect to OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter (Gemini, LLaMA, Mistral), Azure OpenAI, or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
  • Date Range & Store Filtering – Filter all data by custom date range and by individual store in multi-store setups.

docs.title: General Features
docs.text:
• Quick Date Shortcuts – One-click "Last 7 days" and "Last 30 days" buttons for fast date range switching.
  • Suggested Questions – Pre-built prompt chips in Ask AI help new users get started instantly.
  • Clear Chat – Clear your entire conversation history with a single button, removing records from the database.
  • Privacy Control – A dedicated setting controls whether store metrics are transmitted to external AI providers. Raw customer PII is never sent.
  • Test Connection – Verify your AI provider credentials directly from the Settings page before going live.
  • Localization Support – All field labels and hints use nopCommerce's locale resource system for easy translation.
  • Professionally developed and fully supported by XcellenceIt.

docs.title: Need Help? Contact Support
docs.text:
If you face any issues, you can:

  • Email us at support@nopaccelerate.com
  • Or use the support form: http://www.nopaccelerate.com/wiki/support

docs.title: Ask AI shows "An unexpected error occurred"
docs.text:
• Check that Allow External Data Transmission is enabled in Settings.
  • Verify your API key is correct using the Test Connection button.
  • Ensure your selected Provider matches the API key format (e.g. OpenRouter keys start with sk-or-v1-).

docs.title: Dashboard charts are empty
docs.text:
• Make sure at least one non-cancelled order exists within the selected date range.
  • Click Refresh after changing the date range.

docs.title: Locale resource keys appear instead of labels (e.g. Plugins.Widgets.AIAnalytics.Settings.IsEnabled)
docs.text:
• This means the locale strings were not installed. Go to Admin → Local Plugins, click Uninstall on the plugin, then Install again to re-run the installation routine.

You can also send us the exact steps to reproduce an issue by recording it using the Problem Steps Recorder (psr.exe) on Windows:

  1. Open Run in Windows and type psr.exe, then press Enter.
  2. Click Start Record and perform the steps that produce the issue.
  3. When done, click Stop Record.
  4. You will be prompted to save a .zip file.
  5. Email the saved .zip file to us along with a short description of the issue.

← Previous

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text:
👉 Download the NopAccelerate AI Analytics Plugin from our store: https://shop.nopaccelerate.com

Step 1 : Go to Administration → Configuration → Local Plugins.

Step 2 : Upload the NopAccelerate.Plugin.Widgets.AIAnalytics zip file using the "Upload plugin or theme" button.

Step 3 : Restart the application.

Step 4 : Locate NopAccelerate AI Analytics in the plugin list.

Step 5 : Click Install to complete the setup. The plugin will automatically create the required database table for chat history.

Step 6 : After installation, click "Restart application to apply changes" at the top of the page.

Step 7 : Click Configure to open the Settings page and enter your AI provider API key.

You may need to grant read/write permissions to the IIS user on the server where your website is deployed in order to install this plugin.

Note: The plugin installs locale strings automatically during installation. If labels appear as resource keys (e.g. Plugins.Widgets.AIAnalytics.Settings.IsEnabled), uninstall and reinstall the plugin to re-run the installation routine.

docs.title: Scenarios of Use
docs.text: The following examples show how NopAccelerate AI Analytics helps store admins make better decisions faster.

docs.title: Revenue Analysis
docs.text: A store manager wants to know how revenue performed over the last 30 days. They open the Dashboard, click Last 30 days, and instantly see the Total Revenue KPI card and the Revenue Trend chart showing day-by-day performance across the full period.

docs.title: Conversational Data Queries
docs.text:
Instead of building a custom report, an admin opens Ask AI and types:

"Which 5 products generated the most revenue?"

The AI responds with a formatted table showing product names and revenue values — no SQL, no manual export needed.

docs.title: Comparing Store Performance
docs.text: In a multi-store setup, the admin uses the Store filter to switch between stores and compare Total Orders and Revenue side-by-side across different date ranges using the Dashboard.

docs.title: Exporting for Reporting
docs.text:
A store owner needs to share monthly performance data with their accountant. They set the date range to the previous month and click Export Excel. The downloaded file contains a Summary sheet with KPIs, a Daily Revenue sheet, and a Top Products sheet — ready to share.

If Ask AI is not responding, check that Allow External Data Transmission is enabled in Settings and that your API key is valid using the Test Connection button.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: The Settings page is accessible from NopBot AI Analytics → Settings in the admin sidebar. All plugin configuration is managed here.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Setting Description Enable Plugin Enables or disables the entire NopAccelerate AI Analytics plugin. Uncheck to hide the plugin from the admin sidebar without uninstalling it.

docs.title: AI Provider
docs.text: Setting Description Provider Select your AI service provider. Options: OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), OpenRouter (Multi-model), Azure OpenAI, Custom / Self-hosted. API Key Your secret API key from the selected provider. Leave blank to keep the existing saved key. Model Name The model identifier to use. Examples: gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-6, google/gemini-2.0-flash. Endpoint URL Required only for Azure OpenAI or Custom / Self-hosted models. Leave blank for all other providers — endpoints are resolved automatically.

docs.title: Provider Quick Reference
docs.text: Provider Example Models Notes OpenAI gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo Standard OpenAI API key Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6 Anthropic API key OpenRouter google/gemini-2.0-flash, meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct, mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct API key starts with sk-or-v1- Azure OpenAI Your deployment name Requires Endpoint URL Custom Any OpenAI-compatible model Requires Endpoint URL

docs.title: How to Get Your API Key (OpenRouter)
docs.text:
1. Go to https://openrouter.ai and create a free account.
  2. Navigate to API Keys in your account settings.
  3. Generate a new key — it will start with sk-or-v1-.
  4. Paste it into the API Key field and set Provider to OpenRouter (Multi-model).

docs.title: AI Behaviour
docs.text: Setting Description Max Tokens Maximum number of tokens the AI may return per response. Range: 100–8192. Default: 1024. Temperature Controls response creativity. 0 = fully deterministic, 1 = highly creative. Recommended: 0.1–0.2 for analytics answers. Request Timeout HTTP timeout for AI provider calls in seconds. Range: 5–120. Default: 30 seconds.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Setting Description Allow External Data Transmission When enabled, aggregated store metrics (revenue totals, order counts, product names) are sent to the configured AI provider. Raw customer PII is never transmitted. The Ask AI feature requires this to be enabled.

Important: If this setting is disabled, the Ask AI chat will return an error message and refuse to process questions. Enable it to allow the plugin to send anonymized store metrics to the AI for analysis.

docs.title: Test Connection
docs.text: After entering your API key and model name, click the Test Connection button to verify your credentials before saving. The button will display either a success message or a descriptive error so you can diagnose problems immediately.

docs.title: Saving Settings
docs.text:
Click the Save button in the top-right corner or at the bottom of the page. A success notification will confirm the settings were saved.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Bundled Discounts Plug-in for nopCommerce enables the store owner to boost sales by offering discounts when customers purchase additional products along with the one they originally intend to buy.

The store owner can create bundle(s) on a base product and set a discount amount that applies when the bundle is purchased.

They can add discounts for bundles and link additional products to the base product within each bundle. The customer will receive the configured discount when buying that bundle.

Customers can view bundle offers on the product details page of the base product. They can directly add bundle products to the cart from this page. If any bundle product has attributes, the plug-in will display a pop-up window for attribute selection.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Features Enable/Disable Plug-in functionality. Enable/Disable dropping bundle related tables when un-installing the plug-in. Multi-store supported. Multi-currency supported. Create and manage various bundles and set bundle title as well as bundle name. Enable/Disable bundle and apply discount on bundle. Add bundle products to cart from bundle. Easy navigation to additional products. Auto update/delete of bundle discount on updating/deleting bundled product from the cart. Discount amount can or cannot be in percentage of bundle subtotal amount. Position your bundles' list on the basis of selected widget zone. Supported nopCommerce versions 3.70, 3.80, 3.90 and 4.00.

docs.title: nopAccelerate Plus Pro
docs.text:
nopAccelerate Plus Pro is the ultimate search and discovery solution for nopCommerce, designed to help businesses deliver highly relevant, scalable, and lightning-fast in-site search and product discovery experiences.

By integrating the power of Apache Solr, it replaces the default database search with an enterprise-grade engine, ensuring your customers can find the right products quickly and accurately—ultimately improving user experience and driving customer satisfaction.

docs.title: 1. Intelligent Search that Understands User Intent
docs.text:
Don’t let typos cost you sales. Our smart search understands exactly what your customers mean, even if they make a typo or use a different word.

  • Smart Auto-Complete: Predicts products in less than 50ms as users type.
  • Spell Check & "Did You Mean?": Automatically corrects user errors to show relevant results instead of "No products found."
  • Relevance Tuning: Manually boost specific products or categories to align search results with your marketing goals.

docs.title: 2. Superior Catalog Navigation
docs.text:
Guide customers to purchase with dynamic, friction-free browsing.

  • Rich Faceted Filtering: Allow users to drill down by price, specs, manufacturer, and rating with instant updates—no page reloads.
  • Infinite Scrolling: Create a seamless "social media style" browsing experience that keeps users engaged.
  • Multi-Currency & Multi-Language: Fully optimized for global stores, including RTL (Right-to-Left) support.

docs.title: 3. Enterprise-Grade Performance & Reliability
docs.text:
As your catalog grows, your site shouldn't slow down.

  • Offload & Accelerate: Moves heavy search processing from your SQL database to Solr, keeping your site fast even with millions of products.
  • Zero Downtime Architecture: Intelligent fallback ensures that if the search engine ever goes offline, your store automatically switches to default search so you never miss a sale.
  • SEO Optimized: Generates clean, crawlable URLs for search results and filtered pages to boost your organic traffic.

docs.title: 4. Actionable Insights & Smart Search Experience
docs.text:
Enhance both user experience and business decisions with powerful analytics and guided search features.

  • Search Performance Tracking: Monitor total searches, clicks, and conversions to measure effectiveness.

  • Zero Hit Analysis: Identify searches with no results and uncover missed opportunities.

  • Top & Popular Searches: Discover trending and frequently searched keywords to align with customer demand.

  • Recent Searches: Help users quickly revisit their previous searches for a smoother experience.

  • Pre-filtered Search (Keyword Mapping): Redirect users to specific pages or filtered results, perfect for promotions and curated experiences.

  • Conversion Insights: Understand how search contributes to product engagement and sales.

docs.title: Category and Manufacturer Navigation not working
Question
docs.text: Category and Manufacturer Navigation are not working on our store.

docs.title: Cause of the Problem
docs.text:
The currentCategoryId parameter used to invoke the CategoryNavigation ViewComponent is always 0 when using nopAccelerate Plus.

This happens because the controller name is not catalog anymore — nopAccelerate Plus overrides the default Catalog controller.

As a result, the navigation components do not receive the correct category context.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text: You need to update the controller reference in your NopCommerce theme file.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Open the file: _ColumnsTwo.cshtml

  2. Replace the controller name with NopAcceleratePlusWebCatalog This will correctly load CategoryNavigation and Manufacturer Navigation while using the nopAccelerate Catalog Plugin.

docs.title: How can I connect to Solr?
Question
docs.text: How can I connect to Solr? It shows a connection issue.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text: First, verify that your Solr Core URL is correct.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: If your Solr core name is Solr_sample, then your Solr Core URL should be: http://localhost:8983/solr/Solr_sample

docs.title: Make Sure:
docs.text:
• Your Solr Core URL does not end with a / Correct: http://localhost:8983/solr/Solr_sample Incorrect: http://localhost:8983/solr/Solr_sample/

  • Remove # from the Solr Core URL if it exists.

docs.title: Core Configuration
docs.text: Once your license is verified, you will be taken to the Getting Started screen. You will have two choices:

docs.title: 1. Quick Wizard Service
docs.text: What it does: Automatically helps you set up Solr and Java on your server step-by-step. It is the easiest way to get everything running perfectly. Action: Click "Yes" to start the wizard.

docs.title: 2. Manual Setup
docs.text:
What it does: Lets you install and configure Solr and Java yourself manually. Action: Click "No thanks" to skip the wizard. Note: You can read our guides on Java Setup and Solr Setup for help.

After setting up Java and Solr, you need to start indexing. The supported Solr versions are 8.11.0 to 8.11.4, and the supported Java version is Java 1.8.0_451.

docs.title: Admin Configuration
docs.text:
• Configure: You can take a tour to see detailed information about all the features on the page.

  • Perform Indexing Via DIH: This option is applicable only when using Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL). If your nopCommerce store is configured with MSSQL, it is recommended to enable this setting for better indexing performance. If you are using PostgreSQL or MySQL, this option should remain disabled, as DIH-based indexing is not supported for these database providers.

  • General Settings: The General settings allow you to configure Solr-based product search, pricing, attributes, and indexing behavior. These options help optimize performance, control caching, and manage how product data is fetched and displayed.

  • Solr Logs: The Solr Logs page helps you monitor the activity of your search engine. It records a history of important background tasks—such as product indexing and data synchronization—so you can easily verify that your store is working correctly.

  • Product Status: The Product Status page is your central dashboard for verifying which products are successfully indexed in Solr and for manually fixing any sync issues. It allows you to check the "Solr Status" of any product in your catalog.

docs.title: Creating a new Core
docs.text:
After installing and starting Solr, you can connect to the client (web interface) of Solr. You can verify the setup and installation simply by running solr on the port you specified. We have created core on 8983 port so we can access localhost:8983/solr/#/ on any browser.

As highlighted in the above screen shot, initially there are no cores in Apache Solr. Now, we will see how to create a core in Solr.

A simple way is to create command (solr create -c Solr_sample -p 8983) as shown below:

Here, we are trying to create a core named Solr_sample in Apache Solr. This command creates a core displaying the following message.

You can create multiple cores in Solr. On the left-hand side of the Solr Admin, you can see a core selector where you can select the newly created core, as shown in the following screenshot.

To check the created core, you can look at C:\Solr\server\solr\Solr_sample location

[Important Note : If your Solr is hosted on shared server then you need to follow steps to make nopAccelerate Plus workable.]

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: How to Delete Core
docs.text:
You can delete core using the delete command of Apache Solr.

Let’s suppose we have a core named my_core in Solr, as shown below :

You can delete this core using the delete command by passing the name of the core to this command as follows :

solr delete -c my_core -p 8983

On executing the above command, the specified core will be deleted displaying the following message.

You can open the web interface of Solr to verify whether the core has been deleted or not.

docs.title: How to Delete Indexed data
docs.text:
If you want to delete data from the Solr Index then you just need to simply run a single query in browser.

http://://update?stream.body=:&commit=true

Let us see an example:

Suppose, I have a core with name "Solr_sample" and my solr server is running on port 8983, then the query will be:

http://localhost:8983/solr/Solr_sample/update?stream.body=:&commit=true

docs.title: Error while performing indexing using DataImportHandler
Question
docs.text:
You receive the following error when trying to index data using DataImportHandler:

com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1433 has failed. Error: "Connection refused: connect. Verify the connection properties. Make sure that an instance of SQL Server is running on the host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. Make sure that TCP connections to the port are not blocked by a firewall."

docs.title: Solution
docs.text: Follow the steps below to resolve this issue:

docs.title: 1. Open SQL Server Configuration Manager
docs.text: Press Windows Key + R to open the Run dialog.

docs.title: 2. Run the Configuration Manager for your SQL Server version
docs.text:
Use the appropriate command:

  • SQLServerManager15.msc → SQL Server 2018
  • SQLServerManager14.msc → SQL Server 2017
  • SQLServerManager13.msc → SQL Server 2016
  • SQLServerManager12.msc → SQL Server 2014
  • SQLServerManager11.msc → SQL Server 2012
  • SQLServerManager10.msc → SQL Server 2008

Note: If SQL Configuration Manager is not found, verify your SQL Server installation.

docs.title: 4. Enable TCP/IP
docs.text: Right-click TCP/IP → select Enable.

docs.title: 5. Restart SQL Server Services
docs.text:
Restart the SQL service so changes take effect.

docs.title: Facet Filter with % does not work
Question
docs.text: When I apply a facet that contains the % character, the facet results are not displayed correctly.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Solr does not support using % inside facet values`. If your facet value contains a %, Solr may fail to process it, causing the facet filter to behave incorrectly.

To fix this:

  • Avoid using % in facet values.
  • Rename or clean the facet data to remove the % symbol.

Once the % character is removed, faceting will work properly again.

docs.title: 1. Powerful Core & Easy Management
docs.text:
• Everything you need to manage a high-performance search engine—without technical complexity
  • Faster Indexing (DIH): Uses the advanced "Data Import Handler" process to index your products at lightning speed.
  • One-Click Core Reload: Simply click a button in the admin panel to reload your Solr core instantly—no server login needed.
  • Fully Automated Setup: We handle the hard stuff. The plugin automatically manages schemas, database configurations, and request handlers for you.
  • Easy Data Management: Need a fresh start? Clear your core data directly from the nopAccelerate admin panel with one click.
  • Admin Query Tool: For advanced users, execute Solr queries directly from your admin panel to check data without leaving your store.
  • Real-Time Updates: Simply toggle "Indexing on Entity Change" and your search updates automatically whenever you save a product.
  • Automatic Database Sync: Supports direct database updates and automatically handles incremental table updates for seamless data flow.
  • Integrated Configuration: A simple, powerful configuration panel built right into your familiar nopCommerce admin area.
  • Multi-Store & Multi-Language Support: Run multiple stores and multiple languages using just a Single Solr Core.
  • API Ready: Includes full API integration support for connecting with external systems.
  • Smart Setup Wizard: An advanced, step-by-step wizard guides you effortlessly through the Solr setup and product integration.
  • Built-in Tours: Includes easy-to-follow guided tours for product basics and admin settings so you never feel lost.
  • Zero Coding Required: Plug and play! Requires absolutely zero theme customization.
  • Global Language Ready: Comes with support for 36 languages right out of the box.
  • Theme Compatible: Fully tested and compatible with NopTemplates themes.

docs.title: 2. Intelligent Search Experience
docs.text:
Replace basic search with a smart engine that understands your customers.

  • Total Search Replacement: Completely replaces the default nopCommerce search with our high-performance engine.
  • Customizable Relevancy: Extensive options let you control exactly how search results are ranked.
  • Smart Language Analysis: Advanced analysis ensures search understands language nuances for better accuracy.
  • Deep Content Search: Searches deep into Meta Titles, Meta Descriptions, and Keywords to find every relevant match.
  • Auto Spell Check: "Did you mean..?" Features automatic spell suggestions so customers never hit a dead end.
  • Flexible Sorting: Gives customers multiple ways to sort their search results.
  • Infinite Scroll & Pagination: Choose between standard page numbers or modern infinite scrolling for your search results.
  • Precision Tuning: Fine-tune the strictness of search results using "Minimum Should Match" settings.
  • High-Relevance Results: Delivers highly relevant, marketing-focused search functionality.
  • Instant Auto-Complete: Advanced, highly relevant autocomplete suggests products instantly as users type.
  • Pre-filtered Search (Keyword Mapping): Redirects users to specific pages, categories, or filtered results based on their search terms—ideal for promotions and curated experiences.
  • Recent Searches: Displays users’ previous searches, allowing them to quickly revisit and continue their journey.
  • Popular Searches: Highlights trending and frequently searched keywords to guide users toward in-demand products.
  • Search Analytics Reports: Provides detailed insights into search behavior, including total searches, clicks, conversions, and performance trends.
  • Zero Hit Report: Identifies search queries that return no results, helping you optimize product data and reduce missed opportunities.
  • Top Search Queries Report: Shows the most frequently searched keywords to help you understand customer demand and improve your catalog.

docs.title: 3. Enhanced Catalog Navigation
docs.text:
Make browsing your store faster, smoother, and more engaging across your store.

  • Solr-Powered Navigation: Innovative product navigation powered entirely by Apache Solr speed.
  • Layered Navigation: Rich, multi-layered navigation allows users to drill down into categories effortlessly.
  • Supercharged Pages: Enhances Category, Manufacturer, and Tag pages to load faster via Solr.
  • Flexible Layouts: Highly configurable options for how your catalog listings appear.
  • Zero Downtime: Your store stays online and fully functional even during the very first full indexing process.
  • Standard Filter Support: Fully supports all default nopCommerce filters.
  • Comprehensive Page Filters: Adds powerful filtering to Category, Manufacturer, and Product Tag pages.
  • Interactive Price Slider: A smooth jQuery-based slider lets users easily select their budget range.
  • Multi-Select Filters: Customers can select multiple filters at once (e.g., "Red" AND "Blue").
  • Modern UI: A polished, enhanced user interface for all filtering options.
  • Instant AJAX Loading: Filters apply instantly without reloading the entire page.
  • High-Performance Caching: Features intelligent AJAX response caching and Solr request caching for blazing-fast speed.
  • Stock Availability Filter: Let users filter to see only what is currently in stock.
  • Rating Sorting: Allow customers to sort products based on customer ratings.
  • Mobile Optimized: All filters are fully responsive and work perfectly on mobile devices.
  • Endless Scrolling: Bringing "Infinite Scrolling" to all catalog pages for a modern browsing capability.
  • SEO-Friendly URLs: Uses HTML5 History API to create state-aware, shareable URLs for every filter selection.

docs.title: 4. Advanced Faceting (Filtering) Features
docs.text:
Give customers the power to find exactly what they want in seconds.

  • Price Slots & Ranges: Offer both specific price ranges and dynamic price slots.
  • Rich Facet Filters: Filter by Category, Manufacturer, Vendor, and Product Tags.
  • Detailed Attribute Filters: Let users filter by specific Product Attributes (Size, Color) and Specification Attributes (Memory, Fabric).
  • In-Stock Filtering: Dedicated filter for Stock Availability to reduce customer frustration.
  • Rating Filters: Let users filter products by their Star Rating.
  • Real-Time Updates: Instant AJAX faceting ensures the page updates immediately when a filter is clicked.
  • Search Within Filters: Users can perform a text search inside the filter results to find specific items quickly.
  • Smart Lists: Features "View More / View Less" functionality to keep long lists of filters tidy.
  • Customizable Display: Control whether filter groups start as "Expanded" or "Collapsed" when the page loads.

docs.text: Please drop us an email at support@nopaccelerate.com with the issues you're facing.

docs.title: Information to Include in Your Email
docs.text:
When you contact us, please include the following details:

  1. Your Order Number:
  2. Store's nopCommerce version:
  3. nopAccelerate Plus Plugin version:

docs.title: JAVA Setup
docs.text: To set up SOLR and nopAccelerate Plus on your machine, the first step is to install and configure Java. Follow the steps below:

docs.title: 1. Download and Install Java
docs.text:
Download JAVA Latest Version from here.

Install the latest Java version. You may choose the version as per your system requirements, but we recommend using the Windows Offline (64-bit) installer.

After successful installation, you can check the Java installation path:

  • For Windows 32-bit: C:\Program Files (x86)\Java

  • For Windows 64-bit: C:\Program Files\Java

docs.title: 2. Define the JAVA_HOME Environment Variable
docs.text: Defining the JAVA_HOME variable helps SOLR locate the Java installation on your machine.

docs.title: a) Open Environment Variable Settings
docs.text:
Do one of the following:

  • Windows 7: Right-click My Computer → Properties → Advanced System Settings

  • Windows 8: Control Panel → System → Advanced System Settings

  • Windows 10 / 11: Search for Environment Variables → Select Edit the system environment variables

docs.title: b) Click the Environment Variables button.
c) Under System Variables, click New.
d) Add the JAVA_HOME Variable
docs.text:
• Variable Name: JAVA_HOME

  • Variable Value: Add your Java installation path, for example: C:\Program Files\Java\jre1.8.0_201

docs.title: JAVA Setup
docs.text:
Click OK. You will now see the newly added JAVA_HOME variable in the environment variables list.

You have successfully configured the Java environment variable.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Licensing & Activation
docs.text:
• After installation, open the plugin configuration page.
  • You will be prompted to enter your License Key.
  • Enter the license key received on your registered email after purchase.
  • Save the Register license to activate the plugin.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Question 1
docs.text: What will happen when the license expires?

docs.title: Subscription License
docs.text:
If your subscription license expires, the nopAccelerate Plus plugin will stop working. When this happens:

  • Search & Catalog pages will automatically fall back to default nopCommerce methods
  • You will lose advanced search and drill-down filtering features on:
  • Search Results
  • Category Pages
  • Manufacturer Pages
  • Vendor Pages
  • Product Tag Pages

docs.title: Download License (Perpetual License)
docs.text:
If you are using a Download license:

  • The nopAccelerate Plus plugin continues to work
  • However, support & updates stop after the license expiry
  • You won’t receive:
  • Bug fixes
  • Improvements
  • New features

Recommended: Renew your download license annually to receive ongoing updates and support.

You will also receive automatic email reminders before and after license expiration.

docs.title: Question 2
docs.text: What will happen if the license suddenly fails? Will it impact my website visitors?

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
If a license failure occurs (e.g., validation issues, expired license, wrong domain):

  • Your website visitors are NOT affected
  • Your live store continues to run using default nopCommerce methods
  • The plugin features stop only on:
  • Search pages
  • Catalog pages … if the license is invalid or unauthorized

Only the administration area will show license warnings.

If your key was valid once and approved, temporary failures will not affect your visitors.

docs.title: Question 3
docs.text: How will I get notified about license issues?

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
You will see a Warning Notification on top of:

  • nopAccelerate Plus configuration pages
  • nopAccelerate settings pages

These warnings explain the exact license issue so you can take action.

docs.title: What is SOLR ?
docs.text:
Solr is popular, blazing fast, open source NoSQL search platform from the Apache Lucene project. Solr is highly scalable, providing fault tolerant distributed search and indexing. It powers the search and navigation features of many of the world's largest Internet sites.

Some of the Major features of Solr are as below :

  1. Powerful full-text search
  2. Hit highlighting
  3. Faceted search
  4. Dynamic clustering
  5. Database integration

To know more about Solr feature please check here . Once you get basic idea regarding Solr, you can start Solr setup on your end.

Let us start with Solr setup on your machine.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: SOLR Setup
docs.text: To set up SOLR on your machine, follow the steps below:

docs.title: 1. Download Solr
docs.text:
Go to this link and download Solr 8.11.0 or above:

👉 Download Solr

Select the preferred file. We recommend downloading Solr 8.11.0 version.

We are assuming you are installing this on Windows.

Note: The solr-version.zip or solr-version.tgz files (where version is the version number, e.g., 7.5.0) contain:

  • Apache Solr
  • HTML documentation
  • Solr tutorial

docs.title: 2. Copy Downloaded Solr Version
docs.text:
Extract the downloaded file to a preferred location.

We assume you extracted it to the C:\ drive and named the folder Solr. So your Solr path becomes:

docs.title: Solr Setup Steps
docs.text:
Hence, Solr is downloaded and Java is set up. Now let's start Solr.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: 1. Enhanced Search Results Page
docs.text:
Filters, Speed, and Control. Transform your basic search results into a powerful shopping hub.

  • Sidebar Filters: Customers can narrow down search results by Price, Brand, or Category.
  • Total Count: Clearly displays the total number of matching products.

docs.title: 2. Instant Auto-Complete
docs.text: Predicts what they want in milliseconds. As soon as a customer starts typing "Cam...", the search bar instantly drops down with highly relevant product suggestions. This helps users discover products faster without even waiting for a results page to load.

docs.title: 3. Smart Spell Check ("Did You Mean?")
docs.text: Never lose a sale to a typo. Even if a customer types incorrectly (e.g., "laptap" instead of "laptop"), Solr understands their intent. It automatically suggests the correct term ("Showing results for laptop") so users find products instantly instead of seeing an empty page.

docs.title: Improved Catalog Pages with Advanced Filters
docs.text:
This is how your category pages (like "Books") look after enabling nopAccelerate Plus. You get an instant upgrade with these powerful new features:

  • Smart Sidebar Filters (Facets): Notice the new sidebar? Customers can now filter by Price Range (using a slider), Category, Vendor, Tags, and Stock Availability instantly.
  • Product Count: A clear "X products found" bar confirms exactly how many items match the current filters.
  • Search Within Category: (Top Right) A dedicated search box allows customers to search specifically inside the "Books" category to find exactly what they need.

docs.title: Enhanced Search Experience with Smart Suggestions
docs.text:
This is how your search bar looks after enabling nopAccelerate Plus Pro. It transforms a basic search box into an intelligent discovery tool that guides users instantly.

  • Recent Searches: As users click on the search bar, they can see their previously searched terms. This allows them to quickly revisit past searches without typing again, improving convenience and speed.

  • Popular Searches: Displays trending and frequently searched keywords, helping users discover what others are looking for and guiding them toward relevant products.

  • Quick Remove Option: Users can easily remove individual recent searches or clear all history with a single click for better control.

  • Pre-filtered Search (Keyword Mapping): When users search for specific terms, they can be redirected to predefined pages such as categories, filtered results, or promotional landing pages—ensuring they land exactly where you want them.

  • Faster Product Discovery: By combining recent activity and trending searches, users find products quicker without needing to type full queries.

← Previous | Next →

You can find it here:

C:\solr-6.4.2\server\solr\conf\synonyms.txt

docs.title: 2. Add your synonym rule
docs.text:
Append the following line at the end of the file:

This tells Solr that searching for NoteBook should return results for Laptop.

docs.title: 3. Reload your Solr Core
docs.text:

docs.title: 1. Search Settings
docs.text: This section is the "command center" for your store's search bar. By configuring these tabs, you switch your search engine from the default database to the high-speed Solr engine.

docs.title: 1. Settings Tab
docs.text:
This is where you activate the plugin.

  • Enable Search: Check this box to instantly switch your site's search engine to Solr. Once enabled, all search queries will be processed by Solr for lightning-fast results.
  • Enable Ajax Request: Turns on "live" searching, so results appear without reloading the page.
  • Enable Infinite Scrolling: Replaces "Next Page" buttons with modern social-media style scrolling.

docs.title: 2. Search Boost Tab
docs.text:
Control how the engine decides which products are important. You can assign a "score" (Boost value) to different product fields.

Why use this? If you want a search for "Nike" to show products with "Nike" in the Product Name BEFORE products with "Nike" in the Description, you would give the Name field a higher boost score (e.g., 10) than the Description field (e.g., 4).

Search Fields: Select exactly which data (SKU, Manufacturer, Tags, etc.) the engine should look at when a customer types a query.

docs.title: 3. Sorting Tab
docs.text:
Manage the sort options available to your customers on the search results page.

Relevancy: The default and most powerful sorting method. It shows the best matches first based on your "Search Boost" settings.

docs.title: 4. Admin Configuration: Recent & Popular Search Settings
docs.text: This section allows you to customize the "Smart Suggestions" your customers see in the search bar.

docs.title: Recent Search Settings
docs.text:
• Recent Search: Check this box to enable the history feature. It keeps a private list of search terms for each customer, making it easy for them to return to previous interests.
  • Recent Searches Timeframe: Choose how long the system should remember a user's search history (e.g., Monthly).
  • Show Number of Recent Search: Control exactly how many past search terms are displayed to the user (e.g., top 3).

docs.title: Popular Search Settings
docs.text:
• Popular Search: Check this box to enable trending keywords. This shows aggregate trends from across your entire store to every visitor.
  • Popular Searches Timeframe: Control the "freshness" of trends. Setting this to Daily ensures the most recent viral products are shown, while Monthly highlights long-term top performers.
  • Show Number of Popular Search: Decide how many trending tags to display (e.g., top 5) to keep your UI clean and focused.

docs.title: 2.  Facet Settings
docs.text:
"Facets" are simply the smart filters that appear on the sidebar of your search results (like filtering by Price, Brand, or Color). This page lets you choose exactly which filters to show your customers.

  • Enable Filters: Tick the boxes to turn on specific filters like Price Range, Manufacturer, Rating, or Stock Availability.
  • Drag & Drop Ordering: Use the "Display Order" numbers to decide which filter appears at the top of the sidebar.
  • Smart Features: Enable "Show More/Less" buttons to keep long lists of brands or tags tidy, and choose if filters should be open or collapsed by default.

docs.title: 3.  Admin Configuration: Pre-filtered Search
docs.text: This feature allows you to create specific "shortcuts" for your users by mapping search terms to exact URLs.

docs.title: Managing Keywords
docs.text:
• Add New: Click this to create a new mapping. Use this to link seasonal terms (like "Summer Collection") directly to a category or filtered landing page.
  • Search & Filter: Easily find existing mappings using the keyword search or status filters (All / Active / Inactive).

docs.title: Creating a Mapping
docs.text:
• Keyword: The exact word or phrase the customer will type (e.g., "cam").
  • Query (URL): The destination URL where the user will be redirected. This can include specific filters, sorting, and view modes to ensure the user lands exactly where you want them.
  • Is Active: A quick toggle to enable or disable the mapping without deleting it—perfect for temporary promotions.

docs.title: 4. Search Analytics
docs.text: The Search Analytics dashboard provides valuable insights into how customers use search on your store. It helps you understand what users are searching for, how they interact with results, and how search contributes to conversions.

docs.title: 1. Search Performance – Last 30 Days
docs.text:
This section displays an overview of search activity over the past 30 days using a visual chart.

  • Total Searches: Shows the number of searches performed by customers.
  • Zero Hit Queries: Displays searches that returned no results.
  • Clicks: Indicates how often customers clicked on products from search results.
  • Search Conversions: Shows how many searches resulted in completed purchases.

Why use this? This report helps you monitor overall search performance and quickly identify trends. A rise in Zero Hit Queries may indicate that customers are searching for products that are not available or that product keywords need improvement.

docs.title: 2. Search Conversion Report
docs.text:
This report shows how customer searches translate into engagement and sales.

  • Chart View: Displays search data visually, making it easier to identify trends and performance patterns.
  • Table View: Provides a detailed breakdown of search performance for each keyword.

Table View includes:

  • Total Searches: Number of times a keyword was searched.
  • Clicks: Number of times customers clicked on a product from search results.
  • Click Rate (%): Percentage of searches that resulted in a click.
  • Conversions: Number of purchases generated from the search results.
  • Conversion Rate (%): Percentage of searches that resulted in a purchase.

Why use this? This report helps you identify high-performing keywords and understand which searches lead to customer engagement and purchases.

docs.title: 3. Zero Hit Report (Top 5)
docs.text:
This report highlights the most frequent search terms that returned no results.

  • Displays the top five keywords where customers could not find any matching products.

  • Chart View: Provides a visual representation of zero-hit trends over a selected time period (Daily, Weekly, or Monthly).

  • Table View: Displays a structured list of the most frequent unsuccessful searches.

Table View includes:

  • Keyword: The specific term entered by the customer that yielded no results.

  • Search Count: The total number of times that specific keyword was searched.

Why use this? Zero hit searches reveal missing product opportunities or keywords that may need better tagging or synonyms. Addressing these queries can help improve search results and increase potential sales.

docs.title: 4. Top Search Queries
docs.text:
This section lists the most commonly searched keywords in your store.

  • Keyword: The term entered by customers in the search bar.
  • Search Count: The number of times the keyword was searched.

Why use this? This report helps you understand what customers are actively looking for. You can use this data to optimize product listings, improve SEO, and align your inventory with customer demand.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Setup core if your Solr is hosted on a shared server
docs.text: If you are using a Shared Server for Solr, you need to perform additional setup steps for the Solr Core.

docs.title: 1. Configure Solr Core URL
docs.text:
• Enter the remote Solr server URL in the Solr Core URL field.
  • Save the configuration page.

docs.title: 2. Run Core Setup
docs.text:
• Click Core Setup.
  • It may take some time.
  • After completion:
  • ✔ Managed Schema
  • ✔ Core Stream Body
  • ✔ Pattern Handler
  • ❌ DB Config File
  • ❌ Request Handler
  • The red cross icons indicate some manual setup is required.

docs.title: Manual Setup Steps for Remote Solr Hosting
1. Download required setup files
docs.text:
• Go to: nopAccelerate Plus → nopAccelerate+ Search → Configure
  • Find Setup files download
  • Click the download button to get a ZIP containing required files.

docs.title: 2. Copy configuration files
docs.text:
Unzip the file — you will find 3 files:

  • db-data-config.xml
  • solrconfig.xml
  • synonyms.txt

Copy these files into the conf folder of your Solr data directory.

After copying, Reload the Core from Solr Admin.

docs.title: 3. Install Microsoft JDBC Driver
docs.text:
Solr needs the MS SQL JDBC driver to connect to SQL Server.

  • Copy the file: sqljdbc42.jar
  • Paste it into Solr’s dataimporthandler/lib folder.

Example (Windows): C:\solr-7.5.0\contrib\dataimporthandler\lib

Create the folder if it does not exist.

docs.title: 4. Reload Core from nopAccelerate
docs.text:
• Go back to the plugin Configure page.
  • Click Reload to apply all manual changes.

docs.title: 5. Verify Core Setup
docs.text:
Return to Core Setup: - This time the Request Handler should show ✔ correctly done. - Manually update DbConfigFileStatus to true. - Perform Indexing.

If indexing works successfully, your remote Solr core setup is complete.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: 1.1 Windows Setup
docs.text:
Create directory: C:\Docker-data\Solr-volume

Inside this directory create: C:\Docker-data\Solr-volume\lib

Place JDBC driver inside: sqljdbc822.jar

Note: This driver is required for SQL Server integration using Solr DataImportHandler.

docs.title: 2.1 Directory Creation
docs.text: Create directory: mkdir -p /root/solr/solr-data/lib

docs.title: 2.2 Permissions (IMPORTANT)
docs.text:
chown -R 8983:8983 /root/solr/solr-data
chmod -R 755 /root/solr/solr-data

Explanation: Solr container runs as UID 8983. Without permission fixes, Solr cannot write data.

docs.title: 3.1 Windows docker-compose.yml
docs.text:
version: '3.8'
services:
  solr:
    image: solr:8.11
    container_name: solr_server
    ports:
      - "8983:8983"
    environment:
      - SOLR_HOME=/var/solr/data
    volumes:
      - "C:/Docker-data/Solr-volume:/var/solr/data"
    command: solr-foreground
    restart: unless-stopped

Run: docker compose up -d

docs.title: 3.2 Linux docker-compose.yml
docs.text:
version: '3.8'
services:
  solr:
    image: solr:8.11
    container_name: solr_server
    ports:
      - "8983:8983"
    environment:
      - SOLR_HOME=/var/solr/data
    volumes:
      - /root/solr/solr-data:/var/solr/data
    command: solr-foreground
    restart: unless-stopped

Run: docker compose up -d

docs.title: 4. Verify Solr Installation
docs.text:
Open browser: http://localhost:8983

Expected result: Solr Admin UI dashboard.

Check container logs: docker logs solr_server

docs.title: 5. Add JDBC Driver Configuration
docs.text:
If using SQL Server integration:

Copy sqljdbc822.jar to: /var/solr/data/lib/

Modify solrconfig.xml and add: <lib dir="/var/solr/data/lib/" regex="sqljdbc822.jar" />

Restart container: docker restart solr_server

docs.title: 6. Create Solr Core
docs.text:
Create core: docker exec -it solr_server solr create -c mycore

Verify in UI → Core Admin → mycore.

docs.title: 7. Create Additional Cores
docs.text:
To create more cores: docker exec -it solr_server solr create -c newcore

Each core will be stored in the persistent volume automatically.

docs.title: 8. Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Permission denied: Fix Linux permissions again.
  • Container not starting: docker logs solr_server
  • Port conflict: Change host port in docker-compose.yml.

docs.title: 9. Production Recommendations (Optional)
docs.text:
• Use resource limits
  • Enable monitoring
  • Configure backups
  • Use version pinning

Example resource limits:

deploy:
  resources:
    limits:
      memory: 2g

docs.title: 10. Stop Solr
docs.text:
docker compose down

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Solr as Service
docs.text:
Question:

How to use Solr as service in window server ?

Solution:

If you want to use Solr as service in window server, please take guideline from here

Things to take care :

  • Solr is not down when log-off from system.
  • System restart should automatically start Solr on same port that we have configured in service.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Solr hosted on Azure
docs.text:
• Create a zip containing coreconfig folder

  • Host zip file on secure location for solr create script to use it for core configuration on startup. These should be accessible publicly.

  • Host sqljdbc822.jar file on same location. These should be accessible publicly.

  • Update db-data-config.xml file with your sql server credentials.

  • They are now accessible at:

    http:// link-to-your-own-configset/configsets.zip -o /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/configsets.zip && wget -nc http:// link-to-your-own-configset/sqljdbc822.jar -O /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/sqljdbc822.jar

Create New Container App Service for Linux Container

  • Enable App Service Storage

  • Added two File Share in storage

  • Mount Storage in Container

  • Update docker-compose settings with mount storage
version: '3'
services:
  solr:
    image: solr:8.1.1
    volumes:
      - solrconfigset:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets
      - solrdata:/var/solr/data
    command:
      - bash
      - '-c'
      - >
        curl http://link-to-your-own-configset/configsets.zip -o /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/configsets.zip &&
        wget -nc http://link-to-your-own-configset/sqljdbc822.jar -O /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/sqljdbc822.jar || true &&
        unzip -o /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/configsets.zip -d /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets &&
        precreate-core mycore2 /opt/solr/server/solr/configsets/nopaccelerate_core_config &&
        solr-foreground

  • Restart container, it should restart without error. If there is error enable logs as described here: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/troubleshoot-diagnostic-logs

  • Solr is UP

  • A solr core is readily created with name ‘mycore2’. (it is defined in docker config file / docker-compose.yml). It’s ready to use.

  • Or you can also create a core using:http://SolrServiceURL/solr/admin/cores?action=CREATE&configSet=nopaccelerate_core_config&name=NEWCORENAME

  • Solr data and config is stored in storage account used:

- This ensure that data is stored and persisted when container is restarted or recreated / across containers when scaled.
  • You can check error log here:

  • Once Solr is up and Solr core is created, we need to update the docker config file (docker-compose.yml) to remove startup script to prevent it to download/extract coreconfig and create core. Because of this script, the existing core configs were being over-written in Azure storage causing the Solr to be unstable and eventually stop with java servlet error.

    • To prevent this, we just need to update the config file like this.
version: '3'
services:
  solr:
    image: solr:8.1.1
    volumes:
      - solrconfigset:/opt/solr/server/solr/configsets
      - solrdata:/var/solr/data

  • Here are the reference links utilized: https://www.getfishtank.com/insights/sitecore-solr-docker-app-service-on-azure

  • We should add a basic IP based authentication to make sure that Solr is accessed securely only by the authorized client. These rules can be added by adding IP Based restrictions rules in Azure portal.

docs.title: How to Start/Stop/Restart SOLR ?
docs.text:
We are expecting that you have completed Java Setup and download Solr on your machine.

To start the Solr Server, all you need is to run few commands on your command prompt. Assuming you have extracted the Solr folder to

"C:\Solr"

docs.title: 1. Open Command prompt and execute below commands
docs.text:
[Note: You can also start solr on system startup, please check steps here.]

a) select the location of bin folder of c:/ > solr > bin

b) You can start Solr using command : solr start -p 8983, Once your solr is started you can move forward for Creating New Core

Here, -p specifies the port number on which your solr server will run.

c) You can stop Solr using command : solr stop -p 8983

d) You can restart Solr using command : solr restart -p 8983

docs.title: TimeOut Expiration Error on Plugin installation
Question
docs.text:
I am getting the following error while installing the plugin:

Timeout expired. The timeout period elapsed prior to completion of the operation or the server is not responding

docs.title: Cause of the Problem
docs.text:
This issue occurs when there is a large amount of data, and during plugin installation the system attempts to create the Incremental_Table.

While doing so, the stored procedure:

NopAcc_Plus_ManageIncrementalSolrProductTable

takes too long to execute and results in a timeout.

docs.title: Solution
docs.text:
Follow the steps below:

  1. Open the file: Plugin > NopAcceleratePlus.Search > SqlScript > Sp_SolrInstall.sql

  2. Remove the following line from the script:

EXEC NopAcc_Plus_ManageIncrementalSolrProductTable

  1. Manually execute the stored procedure directly in SQL Server:

  2. After the stored procedure completes successfully, install the plugin again.

docs.title: Questions
docs.text:
How do you implement a WebRequest for the StartIndexing method?

Solution:

StartIndexing is an async method so if you request it, it will work in background & will not impact your website traffic.

Implementing StartIndexing GET method in a third-party tool:

string webAddr = <DomainName>/Admin/NopAcceleratePlusSearch/StartIndexing;

var httpWebRequest = (HttpWebRequest)WebRequest.Create(webAddr);
httpWebRequest.ContentType = "application/json";
httpWebRequest.Method = "GET";

HttpWebResponse httpResponse = (HttpWebResponse)httpWebRequest.GetResponse();

if (httpResponse.StatusCode == HttpStatusCode.OK)
{
    using (var streamReader = new StreamReader(httpResponse.GetResponseStream()))
    {
        var responseText = streamReader.ReadToEnd();
    }
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
Call this method after your updates are completed using any third-party tool, external script, or integration so your catalog achieves 100% indexing.

docs.title: Catalog Settings
docs.text: This section allows you to upgrade your standard store pages (Categories, Manufacturers, Vendors, and Tags) to run on the high-speed Solr engine instead of the database for faster performance.

docs.title: 1. Category, Manufacturer, Vendor & Tag Tabs
docs.text:
Enable these settings to give your browsing pages the same speed and features as your search bar.

  • Load from Solr Index: Turn this on to make these pages load instantly from solr.
  • Enable Infinite Scrolling: Creates a seamless experience where customers just keep scrolling to see more products instead of clicking "Next Page."
  • Search Within: Adds a smart search box inside the page. (e.g., A customer is on the "Nike" manufacturer page and searches for "Red" → they get only Red Nike products).

docs.title: 2. Sorting Tab
docs.text:
Control the sorting options available to customers when they are browsing your catalog.

  • Customize the List: You can disable sorting options you don't want (like "Created On") and re-order the ones you do want (like putting "Price: Low to High" at the top).

docs.title: Facet Settings
docs.text: You can create a unique filtering experience for different parts of your store. For example, you might want your "Category" pages to have different sidebar filters than your "Brand" pages. These tabs let you configure each page type individually.

docs.title: How to Configure (Same for all Tabs):
1. Enable & Position
docs.text:
• Enable Facet Filter: Turns on the smart sidebar filters for this specific page type.
  • Widget Zone: Choose exactly where on the page the filters should appear (e.g., left_side_column_before).

docs.title: 2. Choose Your Filters
docs.text:
Decide which filters are relevant for this page.

Example: On a Manufacturer page (like "Nike"), you probably don't need a "Manufacturer" filter, but you definitely want a "Category" filter (to see Nike Shoes vs. Nike Shirts).

Available Filters: Price Range, Stock Availability, Rating, Specifications, Attributes, and more.

docs.title: 3. Arrange & Organize
docs.text:
• Display Order: Use the numbers to drag-and-drop filters into the perfect order for your customers.
  • Default View: Choose whether filter groups (like "Color" or "Size") start as Open (expanded) or Closed (collapsed) to keep your page looking clean.

docs.title: Add New Ribbon
docs.text:
• The Add Ribbon tab will provide functionality to create a new ribbon with all details.
  • You can create as many ribbons as you want.
  1. Ribbon name : This is the name of the ribbon and is a required field. It is needed so that you can distinguish your ribbons from each other, as you can create as many ribbons as you want.
  2. Ribbon Start Date : Set the start date from which the ribbon will be activated. This is especially useful, for example, when you want to show a ribbon for a seasonal sale, as you can specify when the promotion should start.
  3. Ribbon End Date : Set the end date until which the ribbon will be active. This is useful when you want to stop displaying the ribbon after this date.
  4. Enabled : When enabled, the ribbon is available for display on the Home page.
  5. Display Order : A lower number indicates a higher priority. Set the priority of the product ribbon accordingly.
  6. Mark As New : Check or uncheck to enable or disable. The ribbon will apply to products marked as new (applies to new products in nopCommerce).
  7. Do not Display More Ribbon after this : If multiple ribbons could be shown for a product, checking this option ensures that no other ribbon is displayed after this one.
  8. Limited to Store : Use this option to limit the ribbon to a specific store. If you have multiple stores, select one or more from the list. Leave this field empty if you do not want to limit it to any store.

docs.text:
Once the ribbon is successfully created, a new tab named Add Product will be displayed with an empty grid and an option to use the Add New Product button When you click the Add New Product button, a pop-up will appear as shown below:

The pop-up will display a list of all products and have options to filter and select. After selecting a product and clicking Save, the product will be added to the grid as shown below.

The product grid has the following columns:

Name - Name of the product. Published - Product is published or not that will display.

docs.title: Add Product Picture Ribbon Tab
docs.text:
• In the product picture ribbon tab, you can configure the ribbons that you want to display on the store front Available Product Tokens - The tokens will be automatically replaced with their values for the product, on which the ribbon will appear.

${old-price-new-price-difference-value} ${old-price-new-price-difference-percentage} ${product-quantity} ${max-discount-value} ${max-discount-percentage} ${special-price-expires-in}

  1. Ribbon Text : Here you can use the available product tokens.The tokens will be automatically replaced with their values for the product, on which the ribbon will appear. You can also specify the text of the ribbon. This text will appear over the product image.
  2. Enabled : This setting allows you to enable or disable the current product ribbon..
  3. Position : The default location where the ribbon will appear relative to the picture of the product.
  4. Picture : From here, you can upload a new picture or delete an existing one.
  5. Text Style : Here you can specify CSS styles that will be applied only to the ribbon text (label) element.
  6. Image style : Here you can specify CSS styles that will be applied only to the ribbon image (<img>) element.
  7. Ribbon Style : Here you can specify CSS styles that will be applied only to the ribbon HTML element. The ribbon HTML element is the main element that wraps the ribbon image and ribbon text elements.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Universal SMS plugin provides multiple ways to send text notification messages worldwide.You can send messages using the Twilio SMS API, HTTP API, or Email API.

You can add country-specific SMS APIs or use a single API for sending SMS to all countries.

Store owners can customize message-sending events such as customer events, vendor events, and admin events.

The Universal SMS plugin includes a developer API for sending SMS messages. Once the plugin is installed and activated, SMS can be sent by consuming this API from the nopCommerce project or a third-party plugin.

The Universal SMS plugin provides a concatenate function to limit the number of characters in message token values.

The plugin also supports multi-store configurations. Predefined message templates are provided and can be customized as per your requirements.

docs.title: Concatenate Function
docs.text:
Concatenate Function to Limit Characters in Message Token Values

If you need to limit the length of message tokens (for example, when the store name is too long), and you have added Store.Name to your message template, it will be replaced with the full store name. To shorten the store name, you can use the plugin’s concatenate function, which limits message token characters to a defined length.

For example, {%Store.Name%[10..]} will now return “your store..” and {%Store.Name%[10]} will now return “your store” respectively.

docs.title: Custom Message Tokens
docs.text:
Custom Message Tokens:

The Universal SMS plugin provides three custom message tokens for order-related events, as described below:

  1. %Order.NetAmount% – Returns order total amount.
  2. %Order.ProductNames% – Returns name of product in an order. If there are multiple products in order then product names will be displayed separated by comma ','.
  3. %Order.ItemCount% – Returns total number of order items.

docs.title: General Features
docs.text:
General Features:

  1. Multi-country support for configuring country-specific SMS Gateway APIs.
  2. Open API that allows you to configure any SMS gateway using HTTP or Email APIs. This enables integration with any SMS gateway that supports HTTP or Email APIs.
  3. Supports Twilio Voice and SMS APIs.
  4. Customer mobile number verification using OTP (One-Time Password) during registration and mobile number change events, with a themable front-end view template.
  5. Editable SMS templates for supported events with dynamic tokens.
  6. Send private and personalized SMS messages per customer and per order.
  7. Supports both guest and registered customers.
  8. Supports multi-store environments.
  9. Professionally developed and fully supported.

docs.title: Installation & Licensing
docs.text:
Download the plugin object code and place it in your plugins folder. Then follow the standard nopCommerce plugin installation procedure. The Universal SMS plugin is available under the SMS Providers group on the Local Plugins page. Once the plugin is installed, go to the nopCommerce admin panel and navigate to Configuration → Plugins → Local Plugins → Universal SMS plugin. You will be prompted to enter your license key, as shown in the figure below. Enter the license key that you received at your registered email address after purchasing this plugin from our website.

You may need to grant read/write permissions to the IIS users on the server where your website is deployed or hosted in order to install this plugin.

docs.title: SMS Http API
docs.text:
SMS HTTP API:

  • You need to register with an SMS provider that provides SMS Http API.
  • Here I have registered for “smsgatewayhub” to send SMS through SMS Http API. You can register from this URL: https://www.smsgatewayhub.com/ or you can register with other SMS HTTP API providers.
  • If you have registered with 'smsgatewayhub' then go to the API document → HTTP API tab and get API URL and if you have registered from different SMS Http API Providers then you need to get API URL from them providers.

Get the API Key from SMS HTTP API providers and replace the API key in the URL and replace the country code field with the #COUNTRYCODE#, mobile number field with the #MOBILENUMBER# and message field with the #MESSAGE# respectively.

After replacing the fields, the URL will look like this: “https://www.smsgatewayhub.com/api/mt/SendSMS?APIKey=rLssEX9BxkmIHmR47t1D&senderid=WEBSMS&channel=2&DCS=0&flashsms=0&number=#MOBILENUMBER#&text=#MESSAGE#&route=clickhere”

Now go to the universal SMS plugin → configure page → interface setting tab.

  • Country: Select the country to send SMS.
  • Provider Interface Type: Select SMS HTTP API.
  • API/Gateway: Enter your API URL with the replaced tokens and API key. “https://www.smsgatewayhub.com/api/mt/SendSMS?APIKey=rLssEX9BxkmIHmR47t1DnQ&senderid=TESTIN&channel=2&DCS=0&flashsms=0&number=#MOBILENUMBER#&text=#MESSAGE#&route=clickhere”
  • Active: Check if Active.

← Previous | Next →

docs.title: Twilio SMS API
docs.text:
Twilio SMS API:

  • You need to register with Twilio from this URL: https://www.twilio.com/

  • Select programmable SMS and click on continue.

  • Enter project name or you can skip the steps unless you reach the Dashboard.

  • Go to the Twilio home page, where you can find your Twilio credentials under Project Info.

  • Go to Phone Numbers and click on the Active Number.

  • This will activate your number, allowing you to use it for SMS.

  • Now go to the Universal SMS Plugin → Configure page → Interface Settings tab.

  • Country: Select the country for sending SMS.

  • Provider Interface Type: Select Twilio SMS API.
  • Twilio SID: Enter the Twilio SID obtained from your Twilio account.
  • Twilio Auth Token: Enter the Twilio Auth Token obtained from your Twilio account.
  • Twilio SMS number: Enter the Twilio phone number obtained from your Twilio account. Note: Enter the phone number without the country code, spaces, or dashes. Example: If your phone number is +1 256-645-8461, enter it as 2566458461.
  • Active: Check Active to enable the configuration.

Note (Twilio Trial Account): - If you are using a Twilio trial account, you must verify the recipient’s phone number in the Twilio Console using the following URL: “https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/verified”

Click the ( + ) button to verify the receiver’s phone number.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Universal Zip Code plug-in is a complete, feature-rich solution for nopCommerce. It allows your visitors to quickly check whether their delivery location is serviceable and if Cash on Delivery (COD) is available for their area.

Visitors can view this information directly on the Product Details page, reducing checkout drop-offs. The plug-in also helps you discover locations that users are requesting but you currently do not serve, allowing you to expand coverage strategically.

You can prompt users to enter their zip code on their first visit. Once entered, the plug-in remembers it, so they do not need to check again.

The plug-in also prevents checkout for non-serviceable zip codes and automatically disables the COD payment method when it’s unavailable.

Zip codes, along with their shipping and COD status, can be maintained easily in a simple Excel file, which can be imported directly into nopCommerce.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Display whether you ship to a visitor’s location using their Zip code.
  • Show whether COD (Cash on Delivery) is available for their Zip code.
  • Manage your Zip code list from the admin panel, with bulk Excel import/export. Configure each Zip code to allow Shipping, COD, or both.
  • Automatically prompt new visitors for their Zip code via a pop-up on their first visit.
  • Restrict COD (or any selected payment method) if COD is not available for the entered Zip code.
  • Disable the Add to Cart button when shipping is not available for the entered Zip code (optional).
  • Disable Cart Checkout when shipping is unavailable and the Add to Cart button is still enabled (optional).
  • Fast Zip code lookup via Ajax — no performance impact on your store.
  • Remember the last-checked Zip code on product detail pages using cookies.
  • Supports both Single Product and Grouped Product pages.
  • Configurable placement of the Zip code component on product pages.
  • Fully customizable design files for the Zip code UI.
  • Fast bulk import/export of unlimited Zip codes using Excel files.
  • Track unserviceable Zip codes that visitors attempt to use. Export logs for attempted-but-unserved locations.
  • Compatible with any third-party one-page checkout plugin (tested with nop-template’s One Page Checkout).
  • Multi-store support.
  • Supported nopCommerce versions: 3.50 to 4.90.

← Previous | Next →

docs.text:

  • Disable Add to Cart button if shipping is not available : Enable to hide the Add to Cart button when shipping is not available for the entered Zip code.

  • Disable checkout if shipping is not available : Enable to block checkout when shipping isn’t available for any selected product.

  • Auto ask zipcode via pop-up on homepage : Enable to automatically prompt new visitors for their Zip code on the homepage.

  • Disable payment method if COD is not available : Select which payment method should be disabled when COD is unavailable.

  • Select WidgetZone for Product : Select the widget zone to display the zip code checker on the product page.

docs.title: Add bundled products
docs.text:
You can add one or more products to the bundle by selecting the checkboxes for the products you want to include.

You can filter products using the fields provided above the product list.

The product list shows items that are eligible to be added to a bundle. Products appear in the list when they meet the following criteria:

  • The product is not already part of the selected bundle.
  • The product is not the main (base) product of the bundle.
  • The product is a standard product type (not grouped).
  • The product is not configured as a gift card.
  • The product uses a fixed price set by the store.
  • For vendor users, the product belongs to your account.

Note: Custom products are not supported by this plugin.

If none of the above conditions apply, please contact our support team. Refer to How to get help. for assistance.

docs.text:
• Step 1: Go to Admin Panel.

  • Step 3: Select the product you want to add to the bundle.
  • Step 4: Click Edit to open the Product Edit page.

Once the Product Edit page opens, you will see the Manage Bundles tab as shown below.

When you click this tab, a page will appear where you can create, manage, and configure product bundles.

Manage Bundles

The Manage Bundles tab will not be displayed if the product falls under any of the following conditions, as these scenarios are not supported by the plugin:

  • The product is a Grouped Product.
  • The product is a Gift Card.

If none of the above conditions apply to your product, you can get help from our team. Please refer How to get help.
